Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
All measured parameters are within normal limits except for a mildly elevated chloride, which is likely of minimal clinical significance in the absence of other electrolyte disturbances. Renal function, glycemic control, and lipid profile are unremarkable. No evidence of microalbuminuria. Overall, laboratory findings are stable and do not indicate acute pathology.
